name: Parallel Heels
setup: Lying supine, heels placed on PTBar, hip width apart, legs bent

movement.action: Straighten legs
Bend legs, return to start position

cues: Keep feet dorsiflexed and stable as if standing on floor
Reach heels toward ceiling when straightening legs

name: Parallel Toes
setup: Lying supine, toes placed on PTBar, feet hip width apart, legs bent

movement.action: Straighten legs
Bend legs, return to start position

cues: Establish maximum plantarflexion when legs are straight
Keep heels stable, moving around ankle joint

name: V-Position Toes
setup: Lying supine, toes placed on PTBar, heels together, feet in small V position, legs bent

movement.action: Straighten legs
Bend legs, return to start position

cues: Establish plantarflexion when legs are straight
Keep heels stable, moving around ankle joint

name: Open V-Position Heels
setup: Lying supine, hips externally rotated, heels placed on outer edge of PTBar, toes under sides of PTBar, wide V position, legs bent

movement.action: Straighten legs
Bend legs, return to start position

cues: Reach heels toward ceiling when straightening legs
Avoid excessive knee flexion (maximum of 90 degrees)

name: Open V-Position Toes
setup: Lying supine, legs externally rotated, toes placed on outer edge of PTBar, wide V position, legs bent

movement.action: Straighten legs
Bend legs, return to start position

cues: Establish maximum plantarflexion when legs are straight
Keep heels stable, moving around ankle joint

name: Calf Raises
setup: Lying supine, toes placed on PTBar, feet hip width apart and plantarflexed, legs straight and parallel

movement.action: Dorsiflex feet
Plantarflex feet, return to start position

cues: Use full range of motion of ankle joint

name: Prances
setup: Lying supine, toes placed on PTBar, feet hip width apart and plantarflexed, legs straight and parallel

movement.action: Dorsiflex one foot and plantarflex other simultaneously, repeat
Dorsiflex one foot and plantarflex other simultaneously, repeat

cues: Keep both feet equally weighted and active
Plantarflex feet and straighten legs completely between each repetition
Use full range of motion of ankle joint

name: Single Leg Heel
setup: Lying supine, heel of one foot placed on PTBar, leg bent, other leg placed on mat, leg straight, foot plantarflexed

movement.action: Straighten leg
Bend leg, return to start position

cues: Reach heel toward ceiling when straightening leg

name: Single Leg Toes
setup: Lying supine, toes of one foot placed on PTBar, leg bent, other leg placed on mat, leg straight, foot plantarflexed

movement.action: Straighten leg
Bend leg, return to start position

cues: Establish maximum plantarflexion when leg is straight
Keep heel stable, moving around ankle joint

name: Roll-Up with RUBar
setup: Sitting upright, crossbar at eye level, legs straight and together, feet plantarflexed, hands holding RUBar, arms straight and shoulder width apart, round trunk into C Curve

movement.action: Roll down to neutral spine position
Lift head and chest
Roll up, return to start position
Pause

cues: Maintain C curve position when rolling down and rolling up
Pause when shoulders are in front of hip joints and eyes over knee joints

name: Spine Twist Supine with PTBar
setup: Lying supine, lumbar spine imprinted in mat, shoulders under PTBar, arms straight in line with shoulders, hands holding PTBar, legs in tabletop position

movement.action: Lower legs to one side
Lift legs to center, return to start position

cues: Move legs and pelvis as one unit

name: Mini Roll-Up
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, legs bent and parallel, feet hip width apart, arms straight and shoulder width apart, hands holding PTBar, lift to Chest Lift position, shoulders under PTBar

movement.action: Lift trunk
Lower trunk, return to start position
Lower trunk to supine position

cues: Maintain C curve position while lifting and lowering

name: Mini Roll-Up Oblique
setup: Start in Mini Roll-Up position, one hand holding PTBar palm facing up aligned with shoulder, other hand behind head, lift to Chest Lift position, rotate trunk to Chest Lift with Rotation position

movement.action: Lift trunk
Lower trunk, return to start position
Return to center, lower trunk to supine position

cues: Maximize rotation while avoiding lateral flexion

name: Roll-Up with PTBar
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, arms straight and shoulder width apart, hands holding PTBar, legs straight and together, feet plantarflexed

movement.action: Lift head and chest
Roll up to upright sitting position, arms overhead
Bend arms, straighten arms
Roll down, return to start position

objectives: Spinal flexor and spinal extensor control
cues: Bend arms until PTBar is aligned with top of head
Maintain slight shoulder external rotation and reach elbows out to sides when bending arms

name: Frog
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, hips externally rotated, feet in straps, dorsiflexed, heels together, hips and knees at 90 degrees, hands holding F2 handles arms bent at 90 degrees in elbow and shoulder joints or arms straight overhead, holding poles thumbs wrapped around

movement.action: Straighten legs
Bend legs, return to start position

cues: Keep heels traveling along horizontal line

name: Circles Down
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, legs straight perpendicular to floor, hips externally rotated, feet in straps, plantarflexed, hands holding F2 handles arms bent at 90 degrees in elbow and shoulder joints or arms straight overhead, holding poles thumbs wrapped around

movement.action: Lower legs
Open legs and circle around, lift legs, return to start position

cues: Draw straight line down center, squeezing heels together
Draw two semi-circles back to back as legs circle around

name: Circles Up
setup: Starting in Circles Down position

movement.action: Open legs out to sides, circle legs around and together
Lift legs, return to start position

cues: Draw straight line up center, squeezing heels together
Draw two semi-circles back to back as legs circle around

name: Walking
setup: Starting in Circles Down position, legs parallel

movement.action: Lower legs with flutter kick motion
Lift legs with flutter kick motion, return to start position

cues: Keep movement small, consistent, and brisk

name: Bicycle
setup: Starting in Walking position

movement.action: Lower one leg to mat into L shape, bend knee to 90 degrees and flex hip bringing leg toward chest, straighten leg to ceiling; simultaneously repeat with other leg
Continue alternating bicycle pattern with both legs

cues: Establish L shape with legs in each cycle
Keep legs parallel avoiding hip external rotation

name: Frog
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, legs parallel, one foot in strap, dorsiflexed, hip and knee at 90 degrees, other leg placed on mat, leg straight, foot plantarflexed, hands holding F2 handles arms bent at 90 degrees in elbow and shoulder joints or arms straight overhead, holding poles thumbs wrapped around

movement.action: Straighten leg
Bend leg, return to start position

cues: Keep heel traveling along horizontal line
Keep supporting leg elongated and stable

name: Circles Down
setup: Starting in Frog position, leg perpendicular to mat, foot plantarflexed

movement.action: Lower leg
Open leg and circle around, lift leg, return to start position

cues: Draw semi-circle as leg circles down, around and up
Keep supporting leg elongated and stable

name: Circles Up
setup: Starting in Circles Down position

movement.action: Open leg out to side, circle leg around and together
Lift leg, return to start position

cues: Draw semi-circle as leg circles out, around and up
Keep supporting leg elongated and stable

name: Hip Extension
setup: Starting in Circles Down position

movement.action: Lower leg
Lift leg, return to start position

cues: Keep supporting leg elongated and stable

name: Bicycle
setup: Starting in Circles Down position

movement.action: Lower leg
Bend knee, flex hip bringing leg toward chest, straighten leg toward ceiling, return to start position

cues: Establish L shape with leg in each cycle
Keep leg parallel avoiding hip external rotation
Keep supporting leg elongated and stable

name: Monkey
setup: Lying supine, arms straight and shoulder width apart, hands holding PTBar, legs bent, toes placed on PTBar, feet hip width apart and plantarflexed

movement.action: Roll up, simultaneously straighten legs
Dorsiflex and plantarflex feet
Roll down, simultaneously bend legs, return to start position

objectives: Hip extensor and ankle-foot plantarflexor stretch
cues: Maintain plantarflexion while rolling up and down
Engage back extensors in pike position

name: Tower Prep
setup: Lying supine, arms straight and overhead, hands holding poles (close to mat), legs straight and hip width apart, toes placed on PTBar and plantarflexed

movement.action: Roll up onto shoulder girdle, simultaneously bend legs and extend hips
Pause
Roll down, simultaneously straighten legs
Dorsiflex and plantarflex feet

cues: Keep arms straight and maintain scapular stabilization
Maintain hip extensor engagement when rolling up and down

name: Sitting Forward
setup: Sitting upright, facing PT Bar, feet pressing against poles, arms straight, shoulder width apart, hands holding PTBar

movement.action: Round trunk into C curve position leaning back, press bar down, transfer trunk forward
Straighten trunk keeping PTBar still
Round trunk, return to C curve position leaning back, lift arms
Straighten trunk, sit upright, return to start position

cues: Differentiate between movement of trunk and arms

name: Side Reach
setup: Starting in Sitting Forward position

movement.action: Round trunk into C curve position leaning back
Release one hand, reach back, palm facing up
Return hand to PTBar
Straighten trunk, sit upright, return to start position

cues: Keep foot of stretching side pressing into pole
Keep reaching arm parallel to mat
Allow neck to relax when looking back toward hand

name: Hip Opener
setup: Lying on side in neutral spine position (as close as possible), bottom arm straight, head resting on arm, pelvis perpendicular to mat, top arm holding pole, hips externally rotated, feet plantarflexed, bottom leg straight, aligned with trunk, top leg bent, toes placed on PTBar

movement.action: Straighten leg
Bend leg, return to start position

cues: Keep bottom leg straight and active
Direct top knee toward or behind upper shoulder as it bends

name: Roll-Up Bottom Loaded
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, arms straight, hands holding width of PTBar, legs straight and together, feet plantarflexed

movement.action: Lift head and chest
Roll up to upright position, arms overhead
Bend arms, straighten arms
Roll down, return to start position

cues: Bend arms until PTBar is in line with top of head
Maintain slight shoulder external rotation when bending arms
Co-contract abdominals and back extensors to stabilize trunk

name: Bottom Lift with RUBar
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, arms straight and shoulder width apart, hands holding RUBar, hips externally rotated, legs straight and together, feet dorsiflexed and placed on trapeze strap

movement.action: Roll pelvis and spine up, simultaneously move RUBar toward thighs
Pause
Roll spine and pelvis down, simultaneously move RUBar up, return to start position
Pause

cues: Establish straight diagonal line in top position

name: Breathing with PTBar
setup: Lying supine in neutral spine position, arms straight and shoulder width apart, hands holding PTBar, hips externally rotated, legs straight and together, feet dorsiflexed and placed on trapeze strap

movement.action: Move PTBar down and overhead
Move PTBar up, return to start position
Roll pelvis and spine up
Roll spine and pelvis down, return to start position
Roll up to Teaser 1 position
Roll down, return to start position

cues: Define three distinct phases in each repetition
Keep elbows wide when moving PTBar down and overhead, and back to start position
Use shoulder flexors to assist in establishing V position

name: Changes
setup: Lying on side in neutral spine position, bottom arm straight, head resting on arm, pelvis perpendicular to mat, top arm holding pole, legs straight, aligned with trunk, hips externally rotated, feet plantarflexed, top foot in strap

movement.action: Lift top leg
Lower top leg in front of bottom leg
Lift top leg
Lower top leg down behind bottom leg
Lower top leg to bottom leg, return to start position

cues: Keep tension in springs, avoid lifting leg above 45 degrees
Try to touch foot to mat as close as possible

name: Scissors
setup: Lying on side in neutral spine position, bottom arm straight, head resting on arm, pelvis perpendicular to mat, top arm holding pole, legs straight, aligned with trunk, hips externally rotated, feet plantarflexed, top foot in strap

movement.action: Open legs to Scissors position (2 pulses)
Switch
Open legs to Scissors position (2 pulses)
Switch

cues: Create even V position when legs are open

name: Circles Forward
setup: Lying on side in neutral spine position, bottom arm straight, head resting on arm, pelvis perpendicular to mat, top arm holding pole, legs straight, aligned with trunk, hips externally rotated, feet plantarflexed, top foot in strap

movement.action: Move top leg forward
Circle leg up, around and back, return to start position

cues: Maximize hip external rotation and hip disassociation

name: Circles Back
setup: Lying on side in neutral spine position, bottom arm straight, head resting on arm, pelvis perpendicular to mat, top arm holding pole, legs straight, aligned with trunk, hips externally rotated, feet plantarflexed, top foot in strap

movement.action: Move top leg back
Circle leg up, around and forward, return to start position

cues: Maximize hip external rotation and hip disassociation

name: Tower
setup: Lying supine, arms straight and overhead, hands holding poles (close to mat), legs straight and hip width apart, toes placed on PTBar and plantarflexed

movement.action: Roll up onto shoulder girdle
Bend and straighten legs
Roll down, return to start position
Dorsiflex feet, plantarflex feet

cues: Keep arms straight and maintain scapular stabilization
Anchor sacrum on mat (as close as possible) in start position

name: Shoulder Stretch Prone
setup: Lying prone, legs straight and together, forehead resting on one hand, other hand holding PTBar, palm facing back (away from head), shoulder internally rotated

movement.action: Externally rotate shoulder, lifting PTBar
Internally rotate shoulder, lowering PTBar

objectives: Shoulder internal rotator control and stretch
Shoulder external rotator control and stretch
cues: Maximize rotational movement of glenohumeral joint
Move slowly through full range of motion

name: Cat Stretch Kneeling
setup: Kneeling in upright position, legs hip width apart, arms bent, hands placed on PTBar, shoulder width apart, elbows out to sides, PTBar at shoulder height

movement.action: Straighten arms, lowering PTBar down
Roll down, extend spine into neutral position
Pause
Roll up, bend arms, return to start position

cues: Engage shoulder extensors to help maintain position
Keep arms straight and trunk parallel to mat

name: Teaser 1
setup: Starting in Teaser 1 position, arms straight and shoulder width apart, hands holding PTBar

movement.action: Roll down
Roll up, return to start position

cues: Articulate through spine when rolling down and up
Use shoulder flexors to assist in establishing V position

name: Chest Expansion
setup: Standing upright, facing Cadillac, arms straight and shoulder width apart, hands holding handles (fist or open hand), palms facing back, crossbar at eye level

movement.action: Move arms back
Move arms forward, return to start position

cues: Move arms 20 degrees forward and 20 degrees back of trunk

name: Hug-a-Tree
setup: Standing leaning forward, facing away from Cadillac, arms in T position, hands holding handles (fist or open hand), micro-bend in elbows, palms facing forward

movement.action: Move arms forward
Move arms back, return to start position

objectives: Shoulder horizontal adductor strength and stretch
cues: Keep leaning into springs, maintaining consistent diagonal line
Move arms forward until parallel with each other
Move arms back within range of scapular stabilization

name: Circles Up
setup: Starting in Hug-a-Tree position, facing away from Cadillac, arms in T position, hands holding handles, micro-bend in elbows, palms facing forward

movement.action: Move arms forward until parallel with each other
Rotate arms, palms facing down, lift arms overhead, circle out to sides, return to start position

cues: Keep leaning into springs, maintaining consistent diagonal line
Move arms forward until parallel with each other
Move arms back within range of scapular stabilization
Lift arms directly overhead while maintaining trunk stabilization

name: Circles Down
setup: Starting in Hug-a-Tree position, facing away from Cadillac, arms in T position, hands holding handles, micro-bend in elbows, palms facing forward

movement.action: Lift arms overhead
Lower arms forward, rotate arms, palms facing each other, move arms out to sides, return to start position

cues: Keep leaning into springs, maintaining consistent diagonal line
Move arms forward until parallel with each other
Move arms back within range of scapular stabilization
Lift arms directly overhead while maintaining trunk stabilization

name: Punches
setup: Starting in Hug-a-Tree position, hands holding handles (fist or open hand) at eye level, palms facing down, arms bent, elbows out to sides

movement.action: Straighten one arm forward, switch arm simultaneously
Repeat movement pattern

cues: Arms move in straight line directly opposite eyes

name: Biceps
setup: Starting in Hug-a-Tree position, arms straight and reaching back, parallel to each other (as close as possible), hands holding handles

movement.action: Bend arms
Straighten arms, return to start position

cues: Keep scapulae open and shoulders forward

name: Shoulder Adduction Single Arm
setup: Sitting upright, facing sideways, arms straight, one hand holding PTBar, palm facing up, other hand reaching out to side, fingertips touching mat

movement.action: Bend arm
Straighten arm, return to start position

cues: Maintain slight external rotation of shoulder
